description: BEGINNER EXAMPLE - the TENSION-mode counterpart of example_modular. Same heterogeneous stiff-base/soft-tip robot, but the tendons are driven by MOTOR (force) actuators instead of position actuators. Use this with tension controllers (e.g. teleop configs tdcr_keyboard_modular_tension, ftdcr_taskspace_tension) that command cable FORCES directly. Generate it with:  python generate.py --config example_modular_tension --franka
_comment_tension: The ONLY difference from example_modular is actuator_properties below: tendon_actuator_type is 'motor' (force control) rather than 'position' (length control). Motor actuators let a controller set cable tension directly - there is no kp / pretension / ctrlrange, only a force limit.
_comment_library: Reusable modules (identical to example_modular): each has its own material, geometry and color. youngs_modulus sets stiffness; damping_ratio scales damping with stiffness.
_comment_segments: Stiff base -> mixed middle -> soft tip. THREE tendons per segment (example_modular uses 4): the repo's tension controllers, e.g. ftdcr_taskspace_tension, assume 3-tendon segments for their Clark kinematics, so the tension example matches that.
_comment_actuation: TENSION mode: 'motor' actuators apply cable force directly. forcerange '-100 0' = pull only (negative = pulling), capped at 100 N. No kp/pretension/ctrlrange - the controller commands force, not length.
